title: Mount Hiking
excerpt: Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetur adipisicing elit. Voluptas, voluptate saepe nesciunt provident error nam.
content:
Before you even step foot on the trail, the most critical part of hiking begins at home. It's not just about throwing some snacks in a bag; it's about anticipating the unpredictability of nature.

You need to ensure you have the correct gear:
* Sturdy, well-broken-in hiking boots
* A reliable hydration system
* Weather-appropriate layering systems
author: Hamido

title: How I stay consistent while learning backend
excerpt: Small systems, clear goals, and focused practice sessions. Consistency over intensity always wins.
content: Learning the backend can be overwhelming. There are databases, servers, APIs, and a million different frameworks. The secret to learning isn't pulling 12-hour coding sessions on the weekend. The secret is showing up for 45 minutes every single day, consistently. Focus on small, achievable milestones.
author: Hamido

title: What changed after building projects end-to-end
excerpt: From theory overload to practical confidence and momentum. The journey of shipping your first real application.
content: Tutorial hell is real. You watch videos, follow code-alongs, and feel like you're learning. But the moment you open a blank IDE, your mind goes blank. Building projects end-to-end forces you to face the architectural decisions tutorials skip over. Deployment, environment variables, and database migrations teach you more than any video can.
author: Hamido

title: Mastering the Python Ecosystem
excerpt: Understanding virtual environments, dependency management, and how to structure a large scale API.
content: Python is famous for being 'executable pseudocode', but deploying production Python systems requires strict discipline. This post explores the differences between virtualenv, venv, poetry, and pipenv, and explores how to structure a Django application for massive scalability.
author: Hamido

title: Introduction to Django ORM
excerpt: Learn how to easily interact with your database using Django's powerful Object Relational Mapper.
content: Writing RAW SQL queries can be tedious and prone to SQL injection if not parameterized correctly. Django's ORM abstract this away. We'll explore complex queries, prefetch_related, select_related to solve the N+1 problem, and how to implement robust database indexing.
author: Hamido

title: Working in Open Source
excerpt: How contributing to open source projects can accelerate your career and make you a much better developer.
content: The best way to get your code reviewed by senior engineers at top tech companies for free? Open source. In this note, I outline my methodology for finding 'good first issues', understanding massive complex codebases, and submitting your first Pull Request.
author: Hamido
